mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. A sustained decrease in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months produces positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors reprice equity risk premiums downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.0% as of June 2008 reflects recent rate cuts from higher levels
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over preceding 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and margin compression.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in preceding 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% as of June 2008, elevated from prior-year levels
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window when UNRATE trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales signal weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue growth. A month-over-month contraction in RSAFS produces negative S&P 500 forward returns as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward within a 20-day window.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 384211 million dollars as of June 2008
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict directional moves with Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and future profit potential. Sustained month-over-month increases in INDPRO signal expanding supply-side capacity and positive earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 48% of periods where INDPRO shows positive 3-month trend, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector health. When both series decline simultaneously, the combined demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in broader economic contraction risk. The interaction captures non-linear demand destruction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 975 thousand units as of May 2008, reflecting housing market stress
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline impact) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) move inversely and jointly signal labor market momentum. When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises, the dual deterioration signals accelerating job losses and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as sentiment shifts rapidly.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50 thousand persons month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137640 thousand as of June 2008, UNRATE at 5.5%
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal produces no statistically significant directional bias (p > 0.15) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation pressure (CPIAUCSL) constrains monetary easing capacity. When CPI rises above 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains above 2%, the Fed faces a policy bind that limits rate cuts, preventing the equity-supportive discount rate compression. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative returns in stagflation scenarios.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.132 (1982-1984=100) as of May 2008, reflecting elevated inflation
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to differentiate stagflation regimes from pure easing regimes with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) represents the broadest demand signal. Quarterly GDP growth below 2% annualized signals late-cycle or recessionary conditions that compress equity valuations and earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns when growth momentum deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11703.6 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2008, showing slowing growth trajectory
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of periods where quarterly GDPC1 growth is below 2% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
